metadata.last_ea_commit: ccdd82b (EA-3's v3 mid-stage handoff XML, docs-only)
metadata.nature_of_this_file: Bootstrap pointer prepared by Guide instance 5 for the Lead Architect (LA) to attach when spawning a fresh EA chat for instance 4. This file does NOT introduce new procedural instructions -- it points at the locked Stage 2 XML, EA-3's v3 mid-stage handoff (the authoritative work plan), and the iteration-history artifacts that contain the binding acknowledgments. All actual procedural authority lives in those source files.

ea_initiation.identity: You are Stage 2 Executor Agent (EA) instance 4, the successor to EA instance 3 at item 2.4 partial. Stage 2 is HIGH risk. The comprehension-gate block at the top of 03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml is mandatory: present a structured comprehension summary AND restate every binding acknowledgment verbatim, then STOP and WAIT for Guide instance 5 review before any edits, branches, or commands.

context_summary_what_just_happened.what_ea_3_did: EA-3 refactored 6 of 13 Item-2.4 Python entrypoint files to the canonical `_project_context.resolve()` + argparse pattern. The 6 files (in execution order): 1. tools/fleet_observability/credential_rotation_reminder.py 2. tools/fleet_observability/welcome_back_digest.py 3. tools/fleet_observability/daily_digest.py 4. tools/fleet_observability/weekly_summary.py 5. tools/fleet_observability/dashboard_maintainer.py 6. tools/gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py All compile clean. Defaults preserve byte-equivalent BlarAI behavior (verified by Guide instance 5: resolve(None,None) -> ProjectContext(name='BlarAI', vikunja_project_id=3); the blarai.yaml overlay file exists and merges to restore the pre-2.3 allowlist). The 6 refactor diffs are UNCOMMITTED in the working tree per the Stage-2 single-commit rule (item 2.9). EA-3 made exactly ONE commit this session: ccdd82b, docs-only, the v3 mid-stage handoff XML.
context_summary_what_just_happened.what_guide_5_verified: Guide instance 5 ran every claim through disk-level verification (per the LA's "mature not minimal" mandate and the multi-file verification protocol). Verdict: APPROVED. All 6 V-recipes (V1-V6) PASS. pytest tools/ baseline holds at 280 passed (matches v0e from EA-iter-2 pickup; ZERO regression). Single-commit discipline (w13) holds. Decisions D1 (REPO_ROOT preservation), D2 (ctx vs repo_root kwarg discipline), D3 (dashboard_maintainer private-method abuse deferred to 2.6), and D4 (handoff timing at 6 of 13) all RATIFIED. The Guide-5 verdict file is at: docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_2.4_PARTIAL.xml Read it -- it contains 4 LOW/MED heads-up acknowledgments (g5-1 through g5-4) you MUST restate in your iteration-1 comprehension gate.
context_summary_what_just_happened.where_you_pick_up: Resume Item 2.4 at file 7 (self_check.py) and continue through file 13 (vikunja_mcp/server.py) per the v3 handoff <files_pending> ordering. Then proceed through items 2.4.v2, 2.5, 2.5.v2, 2.6, 2.7, 2.7.v2, 2.8, 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit), and 2.10 (append-only STATUS).

required_reading_order.doc: docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v3.xml The authoritative work plan for your session. Contains: ground truth at handoff, canonical pattern template proven across 6 files, files_pending list (7-13) with per-file approach guidance, items_remaining 2.4.v2 through 2.10, standing rules carried forward, single-commit discipline, and successor pickup verification block. THIS IS YOUR PRIMARY WORK PLAN.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_2.4_PARTIAL.xml Guide instance 5's verification verdict on EA-3's work. APPROVED with 4 LOW/MED heads-up acknowledgments (g5-1 through g5-4) you MUST restate in your iteration-1 gate. Specifically: g5-1 expects LA-side housekeeping in V0c output; g5-2 corrects file-12 path-label; g5-3 flags cleaner.py:180 fallback load_config call; g5-4 flags legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py:28 load_config call requiring 2.6 disposition.

docs/platform_separation/03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml THE locked Stage 2 stage prompt. Read in full: comprehension_gate block (mandatory), vikunja_access_protocol, v2_updates (2.4.v2, 2.5.v2, 2.7.v2), all work_items, rollback, verification_commands_for_lead_architect, exit_criteria. This file is the authoritative scope of Stage 2.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_ITERATION_2_APPROVED_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml Guide instance 4's iteration-2 APPROVAL of EA-3's comprehension gate. Contains: answer_to_ea_open_question on critical_notify.ps1 (default = no-action with Appendix A.1 citation; runtime-path discovery = STOP and report); the 3 small acknowledgments EA-3 had to emit before proceeding (g4-1 critical_notify inspection, g4-2 scope-expansion- stop-and-report, g4-3 begin-2.4-work intent); proceed authorization with standing reminders.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-RESPONSE-EA_COMPREHENSION_GATE_ITERATION_2_STAGE2.xml EA-3's iteration-2 comprehension gate (responding to Guide-#4 iter-1 corrections). Contains: the 12 Python entrypoints + 7 PS1 canonical enumerations; -BlarAIRoot standardization; C4/C6 commitments; Code-Risk-aware risk labels; rollback acknowledgment; preflight_w3 commitment; legacy_consistency_rule for tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py dual-touch. Defines all 23 binding acknowledgments (16 pre-existing + 7 Guide-#4) you inherit verbatim per i1-2 dedup rule.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_TO_EA_INITIALIZATION_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml Guide instance 4's iteration-1 review with 8 corrections. Read for context on the 8 corrections that drove EA-3's iter-2 gate; do not re-emit corrections that were already addressed in iter-2. Useful pattern reference for your own iteration-1 emission.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.5-EA_ACKNOWLEDGMENTS_PROCEED_TO_ITEM_2.4.xml EA-3's three small acknowledgments emitted post-Guide-#4-iter-2-approval (the g4-1/g4-2/g4-3 acks). Defines the inspection-rule discipline you inherit for critical_notify.ps1 + any other PS1 the expanded 3-directory audit scope surfaces.

docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_COMPLETION_REPORT_STAGE2_ITEM_2.4_PARTIAL_FOR_LA.md EA-3's TL;DR completion report for the Lead Architect. Concise summary of the 6 refactored files + 4 decisions D1-D4 (now ratified by Guide-5) + work-remaining table. Useful for orienting the LA on session boundaries; you can cross-reference but the v3 handoff is authoritative for execution.

docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_COMPLETION_REPORT_STAGE2_ITEM_2.4_PARTIAL_FOR_GUIDE.xml EA-3's full Guide-targeted completion report. Contains verbatim diff snippets, the 6 verification recipes V1-V6, attestations, decisions_for_guide_review. NOTE: file-12 path-label in this report is incorrect (says `tools/_vikunja_client.py` should say `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py`); the v3 handoff has the correct path. Per Guide-#5 ack g5-2.

docs/platform_separation/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml Original Stage-1-to-Stage-2 handoff. Contains the verbatim V1-V7 closure verification block you will run at item 2.7.v2 + per-item V8 (defaults-preservation) at item 2.7. Also restates the original 9 critical_carryovers (acks 1-9 of the 23). DO NOT shortcut V1-V7 -- Guide-#3 ack g3-2 binds.

docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md Living state file. Stage Ledger, Vikunja project IDs, Recovery Anchors, Anomalies A1-A10. Note: Anomaly A4 (multiple BlarAI Vikunja projects) is the source of the blarai_auxiliary_projects watchpoint your Stage 2 disposition should defer to Stage 6.7.5. You will append a Stage Ledger row at item 2.10 (append-only).

docs/platform_separation/AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW.md All sections. Specifically: §0.5 V1-V8, §0.6 R1-R12, §1 C1-C10 (you inherit C4/C6 commitments per ack g4-4), §3 P1-P12 process maturity. Use the canonical risk IDs; do not relabel.

docs/platform_separation/WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md Appendix A.1 is the source-of-truth for the critical_notify.ps1 disposition (line 11 is a comment-example, not runtime; default = no-action). Per ack g4-1 you inspect at item 2.5 and confirm Appendix A.1 still holds. Same rule for any other PS1 the expanded 3-directory audit scope (tools/scheduled-tasks/, tools/fleet_ops/, tools/fleet_observability/) surfaces beyond the canonical 7.

docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_4_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_FILE_7.xml You are reading this. It is a bootstrap pointer prepared by Guide instance 5; the actual procedural authority lives in docs 1-14 above (especially the Stage 2 XML, the v3 handoff, and the Guide-#4/#5 reviews).

required_first_actions.action: Per .github/copilot-instructions.md §fleet_pause_sop and §Execution_Agent: verify the fleet remains paused via: python -c "import json; print(json.load(open('tools/autonomy_budget/state.json'))['fleet_paused'])" Expected: True. The fleet was already paused at the start of Stage 2 by EA-2; pause persists. Do NOT call state.pause_fleet() (no need; already paused). Do NOT call state.resume_fleet() at any point this session (binding ack n=2 + n=4 + i1-2). Function-name reminder: state.resume_fleet() -- NEVER unpause_fleet() (AttributeError).

Run the V0a-V0g pickup verification block (per i1-1 + Guide-#5 ack g5-1): V0a: git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD EXPECT: chore/platform-extraction V0b: git log --oneline -3 EXPECT 9d17e7f (LA's Guide instance 4-to-5 handoff brief, docs-only) AT HEAD, then ccdd82b (EA-3's v3 mid-stage handoff XML) as parent, then 3b9b1b8 (EA-2's v2 mid-stage handoff XML). V0c: git status --porcelain EXPECT (per Guide-#5 ack g5-1): 11 M files (4 inherited Stage 2 paths + 6 EA-3-refactored files + 1 LA-side GUIDE_PROMPT.xml modification), 2 D entries (LA-side housekeeping deletions of old "Temp for responses" planning XMLs), and 6 ?? entries (EA-3 reports x 2 + new lowercase temp_for_responses/ directory + tools/_project_context.py + tools/_vikunja_client.py + tools/autonomy_budget/projects/ + tools/tests/). If counts differ from this prediction beyond LA-side housekeeping, investigate before any work. V0d: Test-Path tools/_vikunja_client.py, tools/_project_context.py, tools/tests/__init__.py, tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py, tools/tests/test_project_context.py, tools/autonomy_budget/projects/, tools/autonomy_budget/projects/blarai.yaml EXPECT: 7x True V0e: pytest tools/ --tb=short -q EXPECT: 280 passed (matches v0e baseline; zero regression) V0f: state.json fleet_paused EXPECT: True, reason "platform separation v2 in progress" V0g: git stash list EXPECT: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 (DO NOT POP) Report all V0a-V0g outputs in your iteration-1 gate.

Per the mandatory comprehension_gate at the top of 03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml, emit your iteration-1 comprehension gate as XML. Structure (mirror EA-3 iteration-2 for consistency): - meta block (stage_id, stage_xml, ea_instance=4, iteration=1, branch, head) - pickup_verification_recap (V0a-V0g outputs from action 2) - required_acknowledgments_carryforward_verbatim: * 16 pre-existing critical_carryovers (9 original + 3 Guide-#3 + 4 iteration-1) * 7 Guide-#4 acknowledgments (g4-1 through g4-7) * 4 Guide-#5 acknowledgments (g5-1 through g5-4) Total 27 binding acks, all restated VERBATIM. Per i1-2 dedup if duplicates. - comprehension_summary: part_a_objective (carry forward from iter-2 + your understanding of resume scope) part_b_work_plan: enumerate items 2.4 (files 7-13), 2.4.v2, 2.5 (canonical 7 PS1 + critical_notify inspection rule), 2.5.v2, 2.6 (extended grep + legacy disposition closing g5-4), 2.7, 2.7.v2 (V1-V7 + V8), 2.8 (BLARAI_PID preflight with stop-and-report on drift), 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit, pyproject.toml MUST be in commit), 2.10 (append-only STATUS). part_d_verification_strategy part_e_risks (use compound labels per d6 / Guide-#5 standing reminder) part_f_rollback_acknowledgment (verbatim from EA-3 iter-2) - stop_discipline: STOPPING. Awaiting Guide instance 5 review. No item 2.4 work, branch creation, or mutation commands until explicit PROCEED authorization. Then STOP. Do not proceed to file 7 work until Guide instance 5 reviews and approves.

ea_initiation.work_plan_pointer: The v3 mid-stage handoff is your authoritative work plan. Files 7-13 in execution order: 7. tools/autonomy_budget/self_check.py (smallest library lift) 8. tools/autonomy_budget/blarai_next_task_resolver.py (single-line default change + module constant decision) 9. tools/gate_stale_cleaner/cleaner.py (verify-only or single-line; per ack g5-3: line 180 has fallback load_config -- disposition required) 10. tools/fleet_ops/diff_builder.py (verify-only) 11. tools/autonomy_budget/active_tasks.py (verify-only or trivial) 12. tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py (LEGACY; per v3 handoff defer ctx-related change to 2.6; per ack g5-4 ensure 2.6 disposition closes the line-28 load_config callsite before item 2.9) 13. tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py (LARGEST -- 19 MCP handlers + project_id required-kwarg discipline; per warning in v3 handoff, may need its own session; if context tightens, emit v4 mid-stage handoff per i1-4, docs-only commit) After item 2.4 complete: items 2.4.v2 -> 2.5 -> 2.5.v2 -> 2.6 -> 2.7 -> 2.7.v2 -> 2.8 -> 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit) -> 2.10 (append-only STATUS).

stop_conditions.condition: If V0a-V0g pickup verification surfaces drift beyond LA-side housekeeping (per ack g5-1), STOP. Report to LA / Guide before any work.
If item 2.8 $BLARAI_PID preflight returns empty or any value other than 3, STOP and report to LA. NO silent regex/yaml adaptation. (Ack g4-5)

If critical_notify.ps1 inspection at item 2.5 discovers a runtime BlarAI path (contradicting Appendix A.1), STOP and report to LA. NO silent scope expansion. Same rule for any other PS1 in the expanded 3-directory audit scope. (Ack g4-1)
If post-item-2.9 verification fails (V1-V7 closure or per-item V8), STOP. Rollback is `git reset --hard HEAD~1` on chore/platform-extraction (level-1 per RECOVERY.md; branch is local, not pushed).
If `_vikunja_client.py` cannot enforce `project_id` on all call sites without breaking existing fleet tools, STOP. Master Plan §D.1 is non-negotiable.
If you discover the registry.yaml shape (post-cleanup) doesn't match what the XML assumes, STOP and flag a new anomaly. Do NOT silently adapt the code.

If you exhaust your context window before completing the full Stage 2 scope, STOP. Per i1-4 + dn15: emit a v4+ mid-stage handoff XML (docs-only commit), then stop. NO partial-commit refactor. NO emergency single-commit attempts that omit pending items.

forbidden.prohibition: Do NOT merge chore/platform-extraction to main during Stage 2. Merge gate is Stage 5.10 with explicit Lead Architect approval per R10.
Do NOT git stash pop / git stash apply / git stash drop on stash@{0}. It is a stale wake_launcher auto-stash, intentionally untouched.
Do NOT edit CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or any A1-A10 governance doc. V8 / Stage 6.1.v2 deferred.
Do NOT call state.resume_fleet(...). Fleet stays paused through Stage 4 cutover. Function-name reminder: resume_fleet, NEVER unpause_fleet (AttributeError).

Do NOT produce more than one Stage 2 commit. The single commit happens at item 2.9. Mid-stage handoff XMLs (v4+) are docs-only commits, separate from the Stage 2 commit; they are exempt from the single-commit-for-Stage-2 rule.
Do NOT touch any file under docs/platform_separation/ other than (a) STATUS.md append-only at item 2.10, and (b) any v4+ mid-stage handoff XML you yourself emit to ea_outputs/.

Do NOT delete or "clean up" the v1, v2, or v3 mid-stage handoff XMLs, the iteration-1 / iteration-2 Guide reviews, the Guide-#5 verification verdict, the EA iteration-2 gate, the post-approval acknowledgments file, or this initiation pointer. All stay on disk for audit history.
Do NOT add tools/autonomy_budget/projects/__init__.py. The projects/ dir is a YAML data root, not a Python package. (Carry forward from v2 do_not.)

Do NOT silently expand scope (e.g., adding critical_notify.ps1 to canonical 7, adding files beyond the 12-Python-13-counting-MCP + 7-PS1 enumeration) without explicit LA approval after a discovered runtime path. Default disposition = no-action with cited audit. (Acks g4-1, g4-2, dn16.)

Do NOT trust EA-3's "I did X" reports without disk verification. Read the 6 already-refactored files end-to-end before applying the pattern to files 7-13. (Per Guide-#5 verification methodology -- mature not minimal.)
Do NOT relabel Guide-#5 acknowledgments g5-1 through g5-4 with new IDs. Restate them VERBATIM in your iteration-1 gate alongside the 23 carry-forward acks.

ea_initiation.closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close: On Stage 2 completion (item 2.10 done), your final closing handoff to the LA must include: - Stage 2 BlarAI single commit hash (from item 2.9) - Outputs of V1-V7 (from EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml verification block) -- concise - Per-work-item V8 result summary (PASS / observed-output-snippet) for items 2.4 and 2.5 - critical_notify.ps1 inspection disposition (with Appendix A.1 citation) - Decision on legacy tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py treatment at 2.6 (route through new chokepoint OR fold in entirely; per ack g5-4 the line-28 load_config callsite must be CLOSED -- not deferred further) - Decision on cleaner.py:180 fallback load_config disposition (per ack g5-3) - Decision on watchpoint w_A4_auxiliaries (extend at Stage 2 vs deferred to Stage 6; most likely defer) - Confirmation: fleet still paused, Vikunja still stopped, scheduled tasks still Disabled, no new worktrees, stash@{0} untouched - Any A11+ anomalies recorded in STATUS.md (using the format used by A4-A10)
